name: Linux Desktop
description: Give Claude full desktop control on Linux/X11 — screenshot, mouse, keyboard, windows, clipboard, app launch.
long_description:
This extension lets Claude interact with your Linux desktop the way Windows-MCP does on Windows: take screenshots, move and click the mouse, type and send keystrokes, list/focus/move windows, read and write the clipboard, and launch applications. It shells out to standard X11 tools — xdotool, wmctrl, xclip, gnome-screenshot — which means it is lightweight (zero npm dependencies) but requires those packages to be installed system-wide (`sudo apt install xdotool wmctrl xclip`; gnome-screenshot ships with Ubuntu GNOME).

**Supported:** X11 sessions on Ubuntu/Debian/etc. with GNOME, KDE, XFCE, or any standard X11 window manager.

**Not supported in v0.1:** Wayland sessions (input injection is intentionally blocked by the Wayland security model), AT-SPI UI-tree inspection (deferred to a future version).

**Security:** Installing this extension gives Claude full control over your desktop — it can move your mouse, click buttons, type into focused windows (including password fields), read/write your clipboard, and launch applications. The scope is deliberately broad; treat it like giving someone physical access to your keyboard and mouse. Do not install on machines where Claude should not have unrestricted desktop control.

tools.name: screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the full screen (or a specific window by title). Returns the path of the saved PNG.

tools.name: list_windows
tools.description: List all visible windows with title, geometry, and pid.

tools.name: focus_window
tools.description: Bring a window matching the given title pattern to the foreground.

tools.name: move_window
tools.description: Move and/or resize a window matching the given title pattern.

tools.name: close_window
tools.description: Request a window matching the given title pattern to close (gracefully, via WM hint).

tools.name: mouse_move
tools.description: Move the mouse pointer to an absolute screen coordinate (x, y).

tools.name: mouse_click
tools.description: Click a mouse button (left/right/middle) at the current pointer position, or at optional x/y.

tools.name: mouse_drag
tools.description: Drag from (x1,y1) to (x2,y2) holding the given button (default: left).

tools.name: mouse_scroll
tools.description: Scroll in a direction (up/down/left/right) by N clicks.

tools.name: type_text
tools.description: Type a string of characters into the focused window (emits individual keystrokes).

tools.name: clipboard_get
tools.description: Read the current X11 clipboard (selection + clipboard) as text.

tools.name: clipboard_set
tools.description: Write a string to the X11 clipboard.

tools.name: launch_app
tools.description: Launch an application via a shell command or .desktop entry id. Detached from this server.
